violations.text: The door to the outside, located in the basement is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2025

The cutting boards on the sandwich prep unit are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. Equipment out of service prior to inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2025

The gaskets at sandwich prep unit were observed in poor repair. Equipment to be in good repair and proper adjustment. Equipment out of service prior to inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2025

Rice, Pork, and Stews were observed held at 121-130 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were assessed and reheated during time of inspection. Hot holding unit was also adjusted to a higher setting. PIC to maintain hot holding logs for cooked foods. Logs provided to PIC. Logs to be available for review upon re-inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2025

The door to the outside, located in the basement is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2025

The cutting boards on the sandwich prep unit are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2025
The gaskets at sandwich prep unit were observed in poor repair. Equipment to be in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2025

An open employees' beverage container was observed stored on top of prep unit. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2025

Various foods were observed held at 108-130 degrees Fahrenheit in hot table and in hot holding unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were assessed; foods prepared around 8 AM this morning were voluntarily disposed of. Foods that were recently prepared were reheated to a minimum of 165 degrees Fahrenheit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2025

Flan and arepas con queso were observed held at 52-55 degrees Fahrenheit. Dessert case was observed at 51 degrees Fahrenheit. Foods that were out of temperature for an unknown amount of time were voluntarily disposed of. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2025

The doors to the outside, located in the basement and the kitchen, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2025
Foods were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2025

The cutting boards on the sandwich prep unit are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2025

The following were observed in poor repair: 1. gaskets at sandwich prep unit 2. faucet and spray hose at 3 bay sink in the basement 3. dessert case (observed at 51 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection; unit taken out of service to hold potentially hazardous foods) New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2025
